visual_checks: Look for breadcrumb navigation showing the user's current position within the site hierarchy
Check if the current page is highlighted or indicated in the site navigation menu
Look for a site map that shows the overall structure and the user's location within it
Check the page title or heading to see if it clearly identifies where the user is
Look for step indicators in multi-step processes (e.g., 'Step 2 of 5')
Observe whether navigation elements use aria-current or visual styling to mark the active page
pass_conditions: Breadcrumb navigation is present showing the path from the homepage to the current page
The current page is visually highlighted in the site navigation with aria-current='page'
A site map is available and linked from the page providing full structural context
Multi-step processes include a step indicator showing current position and total steps
At least one mechanism is provided to help users understand their location within the site
fail_conditions: No breadcrumbs, highlighted navigation items, or other location indicators are present
The user has no way to determine where they are within the website hierarchy
Multi-step processes lack step indicators or progress information
Navigation menus exist but do not indicate which page is currently active
na_conditions: The page is a standalone single-page site with no hierarchical structure or multi-step process
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
The site structure could not be determined from the current page alone
common_mistakes: Breadcrumbs that are visually present but lack proper semantic markup (nav element with aria-label)
Navigation menus that highlight the current section but not the specific page
Step indicators that show progress visually but are not accessible to screen readers
Breadcrumbs that omit intermediate levels of the hierarchy
Using only the page title as a location indicator without any navigational context

examples.pass: A product page includes breadcrumbs 'Home > Electronics > Laptops > Model X' with a nav element labeled 'Breadcrumb', and the 'Electronics' section is highlighted in the main navigation with aria-current='true'
examples.fail: A deeply nested article page has no breadcrumbs, the navigation menu does not indicate the current section, and there is no site map — users cannot determine where they are in the site
examples.partial: Breadcrumbs are present on most content pages but the blog section lacks any location indicator, and the navigation menu does not highlight the current page

plain_meaning: Users need a way to understand where they currently are within a larger set of pages. Common mechanisms include breadcrumb trails, a site map with the current page indicated, a current-page indicator in a navigation menu, or a page title that reflects the location. This helps users orient themselves within the site structure.
scope_applies_to: Breadcrumb trails showing the path to the current page
Navigation menus that mark the current page as current
Site maps that indicate the current location
Page titles that include hierarchical location information
Any other mechanism that communicates current location within a site
scope_does_not_apply_to: Standalone pages that are not part of a set
The styling or visual design of breadcrumbs
Whether navigation is keyboard operable (that is SC 2.1.1)
Whether link purposes are clear (that is SC 2.4.4)
Multi-step forms where step indicators cover this differently
borderline_calibration: Any single working mechanism is sufficient; this SC does not require multiple.
A clear hierarchical page title alone can satisfy this SC.
Only flag when the user has no way at all to determine their location in the site.
Do not fail based on stylistic preferences about breadcrumb presentation.
